description_long: This is a small, bulbous ceramic vase with a wide base and a narrow, slightly flared neck. The rim is uneven and chipped, indicating visible damage and age. The surface is predominantly white with hand-painted cobalt blue floral and abstract motifs, typical of traditional European or Asian pottery. A broken handle is attached to the back, and the glaze has a slightly glossy finish with some areas showing wear and discoloration. The base is unglazed and shows a natural clay color, suggesting it was wheel-thrown. The overall appearance is rustic and antique, with clear evidence of use and historical character.
description: A small, white ceramic vase with blue floral motifs, a chipped rim, and a broken handle, showing signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main blue floral motif and the uneven, chipped rim.
description_view_1: Side view reveals part of the floral motif and the start of the broken handle.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the broken handle and additional blue decorative elements.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays more blue motifs and the chipped rim from another angle.
